title: Company Employee Handbook
content:
Company Employee Handbook - Effective January 2025

Section 1: Introduction and Company Overview

Welcome to Horizon Technologies. This handbook outlines the policies, procedures, and expectations that apply to all employees. Horizon Technologies was founded in 2018 with the mission of building reliable software infrastructure for mid-size businesses. We are headquartered in Bangalore, India with satellite offices in Hyderabad and Pune. As of 2025, we employ approximately 340 people across engineering, product, sales, and operations.

Our core values are transparency, ownership, and continuous improvement. Every employee is expected to operate with these values in mind, whether they are writing code, speaking with a customer, or making a hiring decision.

Section 2: Employment Policies

2.1 Employment Classification

All employees fall into one of the following categories: full-time, part-time, contract, or intern. Full-time employees work a minimum of 40 hours per week and are eligible for the full benefits package. Part-time employees work between 20 and 39 hours per week and receive prorated benefits. Contractors are engaged for specific projects and are not eligible for company benefits. Interns are hired for fixed-term engagements, typically 3 to 6 months, and receive a stipend.

2.2 Probation Period

All new full-time and part-time employees are subject to a 90-day probation period. During this time, the employee's performance, attitude, and cultural fit are evaluated by their direct manager. At the end of the probation period, the manager will conduct a review and either confirm the employee's position or extend the probation by up to 30 additional days. In rare cases, employment may be terminated during probation with a 7-day notice period.

2.3 Working Hours

Standard working hours are 9:30 AM to 6:30 PM, Monday through Friday. Employees are expected to be available during core hours (11:00 AM to 4:00 PM) for meetings and collaboration. Outside of core hours, employees may adjust their schedule with manager approval. Remote work is permitted up to 3 days per week for roles that do not require physical presence.

2.4 Attendance and Punctuality

Consistent attendance is essential. Employees must notify their manager by 9:00 AM if they will be absent or late. Unexcused absences exceeding 3 days in a quarter may result in disciplinary action. Repeated tardiness (arriving more than 15 minutes late without prior notice) will be documented and may affect performance reviews.

Section 3: Leave Policies

3.1 Annual Leave

Full-time employees are entitled to 24 days of paid annual leave per calendar year. Leave accrues at a rate of 2 days per month. Unused leave can be carried forward to the next year, up to a maximum of 10 days. Any leave beyond the carry-forward limit will lapse on December 31. Employees must submit leave requests at least 5 working days in advance for planned leave.

3.2 Sick Leave

Employees receive 12 days of paid sick leave per year. Sick leave does not carry forward. For absences exceeding 3 consecutive days, a medical certificate must be provided. The company reserves the right to request a fitness-to-work certificate before the employee returns.

3.3 Parental Leave

Maternity leave: 26 weeks of paid leave for the first two children, as per the Maternity Benefit Act. Paternity leave: 10 working days of paid leave, to be taken within 3 months of the child's birth. Adoption leave: 12 weeks of paid leave for employees adopting a child under 3 months of age.

3.4 Bereavement Leave

Employees are entitled to 5 days of paid bereavement leave for the death of an immediate family member (spouse, parent, child, or sibling). For extended family, 2 days of paid leave may be granted at the manager's discretion.

3.5 Public Holidays

The company observes 12 public holidays per year. The holiday list is published in January and applies to all offices. If a public holiday falls on a weekend, the company will not provide a substitute day off unless required by local law.

Section 4: Compensation and Benefits

4.1 Salary Structure

Salaries are reviewed annually in April. Adjustments are based on individual performance, market benchmarks, and company financial performance. Salary is paid on the last working day of each month via direct bank transfer. The company uses a CTC (Cost to Company) structure that includes base salary, house rent allowance, special allowance, and employer PF contribution.

4.2 Performance Bonuses

Eligible employees may receive an annual performance bonus of up to 15% of their base salary. Bonus payouts are determined by a combination of individual performance ratings and team or department performance against quarterly targets. Bonuses are typically paid in May.

4.3 Health Insurance

All full-time employees and their dependents (spouse, up to 2 children, and parents) are covered under the company's group health insurance plan. The plan provides coverage of up to INR 5,00,000 per year. Dental and vision coverage are not included. Employees may opt for a top-up plan at their own cost.

4.4 Provident Fund

The company contributes 12% of the employee's basic salary to the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF), as mandated by law. Employees may choose to increase their voluntary contribution.

4.5 Learning and Development

Each employee has an annual learning budget of INR 25,000. This can be used for online courses, certifications, books, or conference attendance. Requests must be approved by the employee's manager. The company also organizes internal knowledge-sharing sessions on a bi-weekly basis.

Section 5: Code of Conduct

5.1 Professional Behavior

Employees are expected to conduct themselves professionally at all times. This includes respectful communication, adherence to deadlines, and accountability for their work. Harassment, discrimination, or bullying of any kind will not be tolerated and may result in immediate termination.

5.2 Conflict of Interest

Employees must disclose any outside employment, business interests, or personal relationships that could create a conflict of interest. Moonlighting (working for another employer) is not permitted without written approval from the HR department.

5.3 Confidentiality

All employees are bound by the confidentiality agreement signed at the time of joining. Company data, client information, trade secrets, and proprietary code must not be shared with external parties. Violations will be treated as a serious offense and may result in legal action.

5.4 Use of Company Resources

Company-issued laptops, phones, and software licenses are for work purposes only. Limited personal use is acceptable, but employees must not install unauthorized software, access inappropriate content, or use company resources for personal business ventures.

5.5 Social Media

Employees are free to maintain personal social media accounts. However, they must not share confidential company information, speak on behalf of the company without authorization, or post content that could damage the company's reputation. When in doubt, consult the communications team.

Section 6: Performance Management

6.1 Performance Reviews

Formal performance reviews are conducted twice a year: in March (annual review) and September (mid-year check-in). Reviews are based on a combination of goal achievement, competency assessment, and peer feedback. Employees are rated on a 5-point scale: Exceptional, Exceeds Expectations, Meets Expectations, Needs Improvement, and Unsatisfactory.

6.2 Performance Improvement Plans

Employees who receive a rating of Needs Improvement or Unsatisfactory may be placed on a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP). A PIP typically lasts 60 to 90 days and includes specific, measurable goals. Failure to meet PIP goals may result in reassignment, demotion, or termination.

6.3 Promotions

Promotions are based on sustained performance, demonstration of competencies at the next level, and business need. There is no fixed timeline for promotions. Managers may nominate employees for promotion during the annual review cycle.

Section 7: Separation and Exit

7.1 Resignation

Employees wishing to resign must provide written notice of 30 days for non-managerial roles and 60 days for managerial roles. The notice period may be shortened or waived at the company's discretion. During the notice period, the employee is expected to complete all pending work and facilitate a smooth handover.

7.2 Termination

The company may terminate employment for cause, including but not limited to: gross misconduct, repeated policy violations, performance failure after a PIP, breach of confidentiality, or criminal activity. Termination for cause does not require a notice period.

7.3 Exit Process

On the last working day, the employee must return all company property (laptop, access cards, etc.), complete an exit interview with HR, and sign a clearance form. The final settlement, including any pending salary, leave encashment, and provident fund transfer, will be processed within 45 days of the last working day.

Section 8: Workplace Safety

8.1 Office Safety

All employees must comply with building safety regulations, including fire drill participation and emergency evacuation procedures. First aid kits are available on every floor. Any safety hazards should be reported to the facilities team immediately.

8.2 Workplace Harassment

The company has zero tolerance for workplace harassment, including sexual harassment. A formal complaint mechanism is in place as per the POSH (Prevention of Sexual Harassment) Act. Complaints can be filed with the Internal Complaints Committee (ICC), whose members are listed on the HR portal. All complaints are investigated confidentially.

This handbook is a living document and may be updated periodically. Employees will be notified of any significant changes via email.
